developerMessage.text:
Recently, Figma made changes to its REST API.

If you get a "429 TooManyRequests" error when importing a layout, you need to upgrade your Figma plan to Pro (full-seat/dev) or higher.

You also need to move the project you are importing into a Pro team so that Pro limits apply to it.

If you receive a 429 error when importing a project that is not owned by your account, move the frames from that project into your own project that is located in a Pro team, and then try importing again.

---------

In the near future, the asset author is preparing a legal solution that will allow avoiding the 429 error on free Figma plans.

This solution will be released as a separate tool that acts as a supporting utility for the main asset, Figma Converter for Unity.

The release will be announced in this information block and on the Discord server.

added.description: Initial release.

fixed.description: Fixed a bug when installing an asset from the Package Manager.

added.description: Modern Procedural UI Kit asset support.
Procedural UI Image asset support.
More examples.

changed.description: Updated designer and developer manuals.

fixed.description: Fixed a #if UNITY_EDITOR bug in BetterButton.cs.

changed.description: Updated manuals.

fixed.description: Small fixes.
Fixed missing download project button.
Fixed cont and img import.

changed.description: Enhanced asset UX.

fixed.description: Autolayout fix.

added.description: Autolayout alignment support.

fixed.description: Auth fix.

added.description: HTTP support.
Handling for errors caused by incorrect Figma project permissions.
BetterButton component: TextMeshPro support.
BetterButton component: Selected and Pressed states.

changed.description: Updated manuals and examples.

fixed.description: Fixed import of InputField and TextMeshPro-InputField components.
BetterButton component: fixed assignment of state sprites.

added.description: Handling for CloudFront errors.

fixed.description: Fixed BetterButton component.
Fixed error in CanvasDrawer.cs.

added.description: Support for importing objects with shadows, without disabling shadows in Figma or using the TrueShadow asset.
Support for rotated objects.
Support for updating already imported frames, preserving existing scene objects and applying the latest Figma project changes during re-import.
Project caching, so unchanged projects do not need to be reloaded on each import and the cache is saved after restarting Unity.
Nested prefab support for Design System.
UI manager for easy viewing of frames after import.
Function to disable sprite compression on import to speed up import.
Automatic detection of additional installed assets.
Ability to move the asset to any project folder.

changed.description: Improved import process, removing the need to adapt new layouts for correct import and allowing very complex frames to import without problems.
Changed the algorithm for determining common frame elements when importing multiple frames.
Renamed the 'Custom Prefabs' function to 'Design System' and improved it so scene prefabs are created as prefabs instead of ordinary game objects.
Automatically creates and sets the required Game tab resolution for each imported frame.
Disabled gradient recreation when importing images with MPImage; gradients are imported as sprites while preserving colors so they can still be enabled.
Completely rewrote the asset code to support technical documentation and company-specific customization.
Updated manuals.
Other minor improvements.

fixed.description: Objects positioning issue.
Issue with assigning non-regular font styles.
Support for having multiple active FCU asset instances in the scene.
Bugs in localization.
Other minor fixes.

added.description: Support for the Shapes2D asset.

changed.description: Updated the 'Problems after import' section in the developer manual.

fixed.description: Bug with importing some sprites.
Import using the Shapes2D asset.
Manuals.

added.description: Function to convert multiple regular fonts to TextMeshPro fonts at once.

fixed.description: Manuals.
Error when importing the asset in Unity 2019.

added.description: Automatic prefab creation for identical components and creation of components from existing prefabs during later imports.
Automatic downloading of missing fonts from the Google Fonts repository.
Automatic coloring of single-color sprites to white for color overlay using the Image component.
Automatic project backup before each import and prefab creation.
RectMask2D support.
Import events for customization, such as adding custom scripts to imported components.

removed.description: Mipmap generation for downloaded sprites to speed up import.
FCU_Button script usage; the script remains in the asset but is not used and will be replaced with an improved version in future releases.
Relative positioning mode with frame boundaries aligned to the Game tab boundaries; Absolute positioning is now used by default so frames are positioned as in the Figma layout.

fixed.description: Algorithm for identifying identical objects.
Many other fixes and improvements.
Saving Figma session.
TryAdd method on some versions of Unity.
Downloading sprites on MacOS.
Bug with font assignment.
Text positioning.
Getting the download link for the sprite.
Converting TextMeshPro fonts.
Import using procedural image assets.

changed.description: Removed the project size limit.
Accelerated project download and processing, tested with a project containing 100 frames.
Json.NET library is now required to work with the asset.
Updated the manual with information on installing Json.NET via the Package Manager.

fixed.description: Bug with detection of Json.NET on Unity 2022-2023.
Bug with project building.

added.description: RawImage support.

fixed.description: Positioning of TrueShadow.
Enabling and disabling of TrueShadow.
Identification of SDF font names.
Destroying of components during project reimport.
'Unsupported behaviour' error.

knownIssues.description: Bug with the display of the asset's interface in the settings tab when a TextMeshPro component is selected.
Error in asset scripts when attempting to build; delete the asset before building to work around this problem.

added.description: Support for the 'Figma to UI Toolkit Converter' plugin, providing the ability to import layouts from Figma into the UI Toolkit.
Support for D.A. Button, formerly 'FCU Button'.

changed.description: Updated both manuals with information on importing sections.
Other fixes and improvements.

added.description: Ability to select during re-import which individual components within frames will be imported or updated, and which will not.
Ability to choose which previously imported objects will be deleted from the scene during import.

changed.description: Updated the 'Layout Updating' section in the 'Manual for developers'.
Moved the Shared folder into a separate assembly.

fixed.description: Incorrect alpha channel value of the gradient.
Distance between gradient points.

changed.description: Improved positioning accuracy for objects with a rotation angle.
Added margin for TextMeshPro objects that have a ContentSizeFitter.
Renamed two tags for Input Field and updated information in the 'Manual for designers'.

fixed.description: Error when importing components using the Shapes2D asset.
Error in the 'UNITY COMPONENTS' tab.
Other fixes and improvements.
Other fixes and improvements.
Bug that caused the 'Create Prefabs' function to work incorrectly.
Bug that prevented the asset from creating prefabs from a previously imported layout after restarting the scene.

Performance bug in 'SyncHelper' components present in versions with a serialized 'hashDataTree' field in the SyncData.cs script; this is automatically fixed for all 'SyncHelper' components when updating the asset.

added.description: Reinstated the FcuButton component and updated relevant sections in both manuals.
Support for SpriteRenderer.

fixed.description: Inaccuracies in both manuals.

added.description: Support for DTT's Procedural UI asset.
Support for RTLTMPro asset.

added.description: Support for DTT's Procedural UI asset.
Support for RTLTMPro asset.

Bug with namespaces.

Bug in the algorithm that determines which images need to be downloaded.
Bug with the addition of extra images that are white.

added.description: AspectRatioFitter is added in Unity when a Figma object has the preserve proportions option enabled. See the 'Preserve Ratio Mode' option in the asset's Main Settings.

fixed.description: Error logging related to the Figma API.
Errors when linking objects in IndexNames and GUID modes.

changed.description: Improved some asset algorithms.

fixed.description: Error when importing asset (name 'UEL' does not exist in the namespace).
Error with zero component sizes.
Exception when creating prefabs: 'ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. Parameter name: path2'.

added.description: Option to import "Letter Spacing".

changed.description: The import algorithm has been significantly rewritten: now, if you use procedural images, UITK, or Nova UI, the asset will attempt to draw your layout using the selected Unity component or framework, avoiding sprite downloads. If you use UIImage, your components will mostly be downloaded as sprites. This behavior of the asset can be configured.
Updated the Manual for designers with a Figma project link, import examples, and sample frames.
Import process can be adjusted using the "Download If Multiple Fills" and "Download Unsupported Gradients" functions, documented in the "Manual for developers".

The "Destroy Childs" context menu function is now available for all GameObjects, not just the GameObject with the "FigmaConverterUnity" script. Do not use this function if you have not backed up your scene.
The "FlexibleGridLayoutGroup" component has been replaced with "FlexLayoutGroup".
Updated the "Manual for designers" and "Manual for developers".

notes.description: A more robust solution for stopping the import will come in the next version.

added.description: Multi-language support for all objects during import; sprites and GameObjects can now contain characters from any language.

notes.description: The Settings window will now open, but the exact reason for the error is still unknown.
If you receive errors in the catch block, please contact the developer.

changed.description: Three items in List<ContentItem> in GetFrameListTool were replaced with a single item.

fixed.description: Fixed regex errors when assigning the project URL via the MCP server.
Fixed a bug in the SerializedDictionary inspector that prevented editing serialized values.
Fixed a NullReferenceException in ProjectImporterBase.cs related to "groups" being null.
A bug during asset import in projects where Newtonsoft.Json is missing.
A bug when selecting a project from the list of recent projects.
A bug where some images that were supposed to be imported as whole were imported as split.
Fixed a bug where some images that were supposed to be imported as whole were imported as split.

added.description: Support for importing from a local ZIP copy of a project (offline import), which helps avoid the 429 error; The feature is already implemented, but creating the ZIP archive requires a Figma plugin that has not been released yet; A notification about its release will appear in the main asset interface once it becomes available.
Support for the new UniText text component, which allows the most accurate reproduction of Figma text and its effects.
Import diagnostics system — the SyncHelper component now displays detailed information explaining why a component was imported in a particular way.
Layout Grid support (disabled by default)

FontSubset expansion: — previously, 22 Unicode subsets for fonts were supported; — 8 more have now been added, including CJK-related languages/scripts: Georgian, Ethiopic, Lao, Tibetan, Japanese, ChineseSimplified, ChineseTraditional, Korean.
New languages for the asset UI and logs: Indonesian, Hindi.
Support for the built-in version of Newtonsoft.Json — you no longer need to install a separate Newtonsoft.Json package for the asset to work.

UI improvements for asset settings: — added visualization of modified values; — added the ability to reset each individual value to its default; — added the ability to reset individual settings sections. Reset management works similarly to prefab revert — right-click a field to open the context menu.

changed.description: 9-slice logic: — added automatic slicing for all rounded rectangles; — manual slice and auto slice no longer reduce an image to its minimum size if it contains a gradient.

HashGenerator: — fixed bugs; — changed the hash collection algorithm. Because of this, during re-import, existing sprites in the project that were imported by previous asset versions will not be recognized.

The asset has been fully moved to an asmdef assembly and now references asmdefs of other dependent assets. If a dependency does not have its own asmdef, it will be generated automatically using the Dependency Manager.
Overall import accuracy, especially AutoLayout and text components.
TextMeshPro import support: — added JUSTIFIED alignment; — added maxLines; — added lineHeightUnit; — added VertexGradient for vertical linear gradients for single-line texts only.
Compatibility with FlexibleImage.
Compatibility with Figma to UITK Converter.
Oriya → Odia (with an [Obsolete] alias) according to the official Unicode and Google Fonts naming.

fixed.description: An issue when downloading images whose names start with a dot.

notes.description: Before installing this version, delete the "D.A. Assets" folder.

added.description: A new solution for the import issue affecting child objects with complex fills when using UI.Image, described in rule 6 of the Layout Rules in the Designer Manual. The asset can now generate sprites on its own that replicate fills, strokes, and effects for such components, which prevents them from being skipped during import. This does not work for all objects, and you must still follow rule 6 if issues occur with similar parent components.
The ImageOverflow asset is now included with the Figma Converter, since some sprites described in the previous changelog item require their effects to be rendered outside the RectTransform.

changed.description: The sprite downloading algorithm. If the total sprite size, including scale, exceeds the limit, the request is now automatically split into smaller batches of sprites, or the scale of individual problematic sprites is reduced. This may increase sprite import time for projects with high-resolution frames, but it makes the import process more stable.
Smarter matching of the corresponding TTF/TMP/UITK font asset by family, weight, italic, style name, and PostScript name, instead of relying only on the exact file name.

JSONNET_PLASTIC_EXISTS was replaced with JSONNET_EXISTS in a significant portion of JSON models and loaders. Newtonsoft.Json is now installed automatically via UPM if it is missing when the asset is imported into an empty project.

fixed.description: Compatibility with UniText.
A project build error caused by the Editor dependency.
An issue where selecting folders in the asset settings saved absolute paths instead of relative ones.

notes.description: Before installing this version, delete the "D.A. Assets" folder.
Support for D.A. Localizator is temporarily disabled. It will return as soon as the new D.A. Localizator release is completed.

added.description: First release.
Support for navigation, OnSelect, and OnDeselect events has been added.

changed.description: Asset inheritance has been changed from MonoBehaviour to Selectable.
The minimum supported Unity version has been changed from 2019.3 to 2020.3.
The manual previously included with the asset has been moved to GitBook. The link can be found in the Manual.txt file and also on the developer’s website.

fixed.description: Missing files have been fixed.

changed.description: The asset's internal interface has been migrated to UI Toolkit; this does not affect Canvas compatibility.
The foundation for localizing the asset's interface into multiple languages has been laid and will be completed in the next update.
Updated the DA-Shared folder.

added.description: First release.

fixed.description: Fixed an error in 'ScriptCreator.cs' for the 2019 version of Unity.

added.description: Added support for 'UITK Element Linker' asset.

changed.description: Version compatibility synchronization with Figma Converter for Unity.
The script generator has been temporarily removed and will be added in version 1.0.6, as it needs to be updated for compatibility with 'UITK Element Linker' asset.

added.description: Added support for 'UITK Element Linker' asset.

changed.description: Version compatibility synchronization with Figma Converter for Unity.
The script generator has been temporarily removed and will be added in version 1.0.7, as it needs to be updated for compatibility with 'UITK Element Linker' asset.

fixed.description: Fixed a bug with namespaces.
Fixed a bug with Parent.Id when importing a layout.

changed.description: Updating compatibility with the latest version of Figma Converter for Unity.
Errors when linking objects in IndexNames and GUID modes.
Updating compatibility with the latest version of Figma Converter for Unity.

fixed.description: Requires Figma Converter for Unity version 3.1.15. Fixed the issue where the USS file did not get populated with styles during re-import into UI Toolkit.

changed.description: Updating compatibility with the latest version of Figma Converter for Unity.

removed.description: Removed unnecessary margins when importing to UITK.

changed.description: Updating compatibility with the latest version of Figma Converter for Unity.
Compatibility with the latest version of Figma Converter for Unity.

fixed.description: An issue where fonts were not assigned to text components in UITK.
Unity freeze during layout import.

added.description: Support for D.A. Localizator asset.
Support for Script Generator.

changed.description: Compatibility with the latest version of Figma Converter for Unity.

added.description: Support for horizontal and vertical layouts.

changed.description: Compatibility with the latest version of Figma Converter for Unity.

fixed.description: Compatibility with versions older than Unity 6.

changed.description: Updated compatibility with Figma Converter for Unity.
Updated compatibility with Figma Converter for Unity.
Updated compatibility with Figma Converter for Unity.
Updated compatibility with Figma Converter for Unity.

added.description: Component template system via ComponentTemplateWriter and <ui:Instance>: — Figma INSTANCE / COMPONENT nodes are now exported as separate template files inside Resources/ instead of being expanded inline into the main UXML; — parent UXML files now reference component templates via <ui:Instance>, while visual styles remain inside the generated template file.

USS Variables system via UssVariableCollector: — colors, font sizes, spacing, border widths, and border radius values are now collected into a :root { } block as USS custom properties and referenced via var(--...); — fill, stroke, tint, text colors, font-size, margin/padding, border-width, and border-radius are now automatically exported as USS variables in the generated .uss file.

Full USS-based support for Figma Constraints: — horizontal constraints LEFT, RIGHT, LEFT_RIGHT, CENTER, SCALE are now converted into proper USS positioning rules; — vertical constraints TOP, BOTTOM, TOP_BOTTOM, CENTER, SCALE are now converted as well; — root frames always receive left: 0px and top: 0px; — OUTSIDE stroke expansion is now reflected in the USS layout box; — rotated parent positioning is now calculated using matrices instead of absoluteBoundingBox.

SPACE_BETWEEN support in Auto Layout: — PrimaryAxisAlignItems == SPACE_BETWEEN now maps to justify-content: space-between; — LayoutWrap == WRAP now maps to flex-wrap: wrap; — CounterAxisAlignContent == SPACE_BETWEEN with wrap now maps to align-content: space-between.
Opacity support: — Figma node opacity values below 1 are now exported to inline USS styles.
Border-radius USS variables for equal corner radii: — when all 4 corners have the same radius, a single var(--radius-X) variable is now used instead of four hardcoded values.

changed.description: Auto Layout implementation has been significantly reworked: — auto-layout containers are now generated as native <ui:VisualElement> elements using flex-direction, align-items, justify-content, and padding; — itemSpacing is now represented statically via margin-right / margin-bottom on non-last child elements, without runtime scripts.

ScrollView generation now uses the native UITK element: — previous custom <fcu:GapContainer> output has been replaced with <ui:ScrollView>; — mode is now exported as Vertical, Horizontal, or VerticalAndHorizontal; — scrollbar arrows and track are hidden; — an additional .uss patch fixes the Unity horizontal ScrollView content direction issue.

SetAutolayoutPropsForChild has been fully rewritten: — HUG now maps to auto sizing (or fixed px for text with fixed bounds); — FILL now maps to align-self: stretch; — flex-grow is disabled inside ScrollView; — fixed-size elements on the primary axis now receive flex-shrink: 0; — text nodes now distinguish between autoWidthText, autoHeightText, and preserveTextBounds.

Instead of TTF fonts, FontAsset files are now used. They are automatically converted from Google Fonts or from your own TTF fonts that are serialized in the asset settings in the "TEXT & FONTS" section.
Auto-width text handling has been improved: — TEXT nodes with TextAutoResize == WIDTH_AND_HEIGHT now receive white-space: nowrap, text-overflow: clip, and overflow: hidden.
GetParentStrokeOffset now correctly respects StrokeAlign: — INSIDE → (sw, sw); — CENTER → (sw/2, sw/2); — OUTSIDE → (0, 0), with layout expansion handled separately.
Runtime USS generation now produces cleaner and more reusable output through shared USS variables.
Component reuse is now more efficient due to template-based generation with <ui:Instance>.

Layout accuracy for constraints, rotated parents, strokes, and sprites has been significantly improved, allowing the most complex test projects to be imported correctly, which previously imported incorrectly in earlier asset versions.
Compatibility with the latest FCU architecture has been updated.
Archive with an import example on the developer's website.

removed.description: GapContainer / ChildAnnotator system: — GapContainer.cs — replaced by native flex layout; — ChildAnnotator.cs — polling-based child annotation is no longer needed.

notes.description: Before installing this version, delete the "Figma-to-UITK-Converter" folder.

versions.description:
FIXED:
• Fixed the positioning of certain elements inside Auto Layouts.

fixed.description: Fixed the positioning of certain elements inside Auto Layouts.

added.description: First release.

fixed.description: Fixed NullReferenceException when opening an asset in the inspector.

changed.description: Changes related to the "Shared" folder. If you are not using Figma Converter for Unity, you can ignore this update.

added.description: A limit on the number of localization requests per frame has been added to prevent performance spikes. It is disabled by default, since no spikes were observed even when localizing 10,000 components.

changed.description: Asset architecture has been changed: the main localizator can now be represented either as a GameObject in the scene or as a ScriptableObject.
The manual included with the asset has been updated.
The minimum supported Unity version has been changed from 2019.1 to 2020.1.

fixed.description: Compatibility issues across different Unity versions have been fixed.

changed.description: The asset's internal interface has been migrated to UI Toolkit.
The foundation for localizing the asset's interface into multiple languages has been laid and will be completed in the next update.
Updated the DA-Shared folder.

added.description: First release.
Preprocessor directives have been added. The minimum version of the asset is 2021.1.
Added example scene. The object search algorithm has been fixed. Added the ability to edit multiple selected objects in the Inspector at the same time.

changed.description: The UX for selecting the linking type has been changed. Now, instead of the two modes, Name and Guid, you can choose one of four states: 1. Name - binding by unique name (works if the name is truly unique); 2. IndexNames - binding by indexes and/or names of the element hierarchy; 3. Guid - binding by unique GUID (the uniqueness of the GUID is guaranteed); 4. Guids - binding by the hierarchy of GUIDs.
The condition that the search for an element occurs by hierarchy if there are more than one element in the Names or Guids arrays is no longer relevant.
Updated promo, minor changes in the context menu.
Updated scene with examples.

fixed.description: Fixed an issue where an index of -1 was assigned when initializing elements of the Names array.
Fixed a build error due to specifying an incorrect "includePlatforms" value. Added missing namespace. Added manual. Fixed namespaces in commented files.

changed.description: The minimum version of Unity is defined as 2021.3 instead of 2021.1.

added.description: Unity 6 support has been added.

removed.description: The unnecessary files left from version 1.x have been removed.

versions.description: • Added binding functionality. The manual will be updated later.

added.description: Added binding functionality. The manual will be updated later.
First release.

versions.description:
• Added a dependency on the Shared folder (included with the asset) and created the asset config file.
• The foundation for localizing the asset's interface into multiple languages has been laid and will be completed in the next update.

added.description: Added a dependency on the Shared folder (included with the asset) and created the asset config file.

changed.description: The foundation for localizing the asset's interface into multiple languages has been laid and will be completed in the next update.
